QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_53

question.text: A company's minimum promoter contribution works out to Rs. 150 crore. The issue is a partly-paid one with calls. How much must be brought in before the issue opens, and when must the balance be paid?

answer.text: Rs. 150 crore before opening; no further obligation
Rs. 75 crore before opening; balance within 6 months
Rs. 100 crore before opening; balance on a pro-rata basis before any calls are made on other shareholders
Rs. 50 crore before opening; balance within 12 months

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When minimum promoter contribution exceeds Rs. 100 crore, at least Rs. 100 crore must be brought in before the issue opens. The balance (Rs. 50 crore here) must be brought in on a pro-rata basis before any calls are made on other shareholders. **The Trap:** The option stating Rs. 150 crore before opening ignores the Rs. 100 crore threshold rule. The Rs. 75 crore option wrongly applies a 50% split. **Cross-Exam Context:** This Rs. 100 crore threshold is specific to NISM Series IX Merchant Banking certification and is not commonly tested in other NISM modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 100 crore threshold is a practical concession — requiring promoters to lock up the entire large contribution before opening would be unnecessarily burdensome.
